prior.title: CDP Supply Chain
prior.summary: ACTIVE PROGRAMME. Annual cycle. ACTION NOW: Check if you've received CDP questionnaires from clients. Respond to all requests — non-response damages score. Aim for B or above. Owner: Sustainability.
prior.what_is_it: CDP (formerly Carbon Disclosure Project) Supply Chain Programme enables purchasing organizations to request standardized environmental data from their suppliers. Over 330 purchasing organizations request data from 40,000+ suppliers. CDP questionnaires include transport-specific modules covering Scope 3 emissions, climate risk, and decarbonization targets. CDP scores (A to D-) are used in supplier selection and procurement decisions.
prior.why_matters: CDP is the channel through which your largest clients request emissions data. For freight forwarders: (1) if a major client uses CDP Supply Chain, you will receive a CDP questionnaire, (2) your CDP score directly affects supplier selection — A-rated suppliers get procurement preference, (3) CDP data feeds into client CSRD/ISSB reporting, (4) non-response to CDP questionnaires signals low sustainability maturity and risks contract renewal.
key_data: Transport module: Scope 3 emissions, modal split, decarbonization targets
Cost: free to respond; membership fee for purchasing organizations
prior.reasoning: CDP is the primary channel through which clients formally request emissions data from logistics suppliers.
prior.full_brief:
# CDP Supply Chain Programme

**Technology Profile** | April 2026
**Primary source:** [CDP Supply Chain Programme](https://www.cdp.net/en/supply-chain) — CDP Worldwide (Registered Charity no. 1122330), programme page accessed April 2026.

---

*Context note: This brief covers CDP Supply Chain as a disclosure and procurement tool relevant to freight forwarders operating across the Americas, Europe, and Asia trade lanes, serving high-sensitivity cargo verticals (live events, fine art, luxury goods, film and TV, high-value automotive, humanitarian). The workspace's role as freight forwarder means it sits in two positions simultaneously: as a supplier subject to CDP requests from corporate buyers, and potentially as an operator that could use CDP data to manage its own carrier and vendor network.*

---

# What's Being Tested or Deployed and By Whom

**CDP Supply Chain is the world's largest environmental disclosure platform for supply chains, operated by CDP Worldwide, a UK-registered nonprofit.**

CDP's Supply Chain programme administers an annual questionnaire on behalf of purchasing-organisation members. In the 2025 disclosure cycle, approximately 45,000 suppliers were requested to disclose through the programme. More than 270 corporate buyers are active programme members — referred to by CDP as "requesting organisations." CDP reports that its platform has 18 years of operating history in supplier engagement.

*Source: CDP Supply Chain Programme page, CDP Worldwide, accessed April 2026. https://www.cdp.net/en/supply-chain — Tier 5, industry body.*

The questionnaire covers climate change, water security, forests, and emerging themes including biodiversity and plastics. Climate change disclosure is the most structurally significant questionnaire for a freight forwarder, as it captures Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions data, climate governance, targets, and transition planning.

*Source: CDP Supply Chain Programme page, CDP Worldwide, accessed April 2026. https://www.cdp.net/en/supply-chain — Tier 5, industry body.*

CDP reports that companies cutting emissions across their value chains via the programme have collectively realised US$54.4 billion in savings — a figure CDP attributes to efficiency gains and avoided risk. The mechanism behind this figure is not independently verified in the source material reviewed for this brief, and the workspace should treat it as a CDP-reported promotional figure, not an independently audited finding.

*Source: CDP Supply Chain Programme page, CDP Worldwide, accessed April 2026. https://www.cdp.net/en/supply-chain — Tier 5, industry body. [Note: US$54.4bn figure is CDP's own claim; independent verification not available in sources reviewed.]*

**Membership tiers operate as follows** (sourced directly from the CDP programme page):
- **Standard:** up to 200 supplier requests
- **Lead:** up to 400 supplier requests
- **Premium:** 400+ supplier requests
- Additional supplier requests purchasable in increments of 50 at extra cost across all tiers
- All tiers include: dashboard access, raw supplier dataset, year-over-year benchmarking, Scope 3 inventory reporting support, and quarterly account manager check-ins

Exact membership pricing is not publicly listed on the programme page. Current rates are not publicly available in sources reviewed for this brief.

*Source: CDP Supply Chain Programme page, CDP Worldwide, accessed April 2026. https://www.cdp.net/en/supply-chain — Tier 5, industry body.*

---

# What This Tells Us About Industry Trajectory

**Three converging drivers are converting CDP Supply Chain from a voluntary best-practice tool to a de facto procurement threshold.**

**Driver 1 — Scope 3 regulatory pressure.** Scope 3 emissions are, by CDP's own measurement, on average 26 times greater than a company's direct operational emissions. This creates structural pressure on buyers to demand data from logistics providers, since transport and distribution typically constitute a material Scope 3 category for manufactured goods and event-production companies alike. Buyers operating under emerging disclosure frameworks — including the EU Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive (CSRD) and IFRS S1/S2 — need supplier-level data to populate their own mandatory disclosures.

*Source: CDP Supply Chain Programme page, CDP Worldwide, accessed April 2026. https://www.cdp.net/en/supply-chain — Tier 5, industry body; IFRS/ISSB Sustainability Disclosure Standards, IFRS Foundation. https://www.ifrs.org/sustainability/knowledge-hub/introduction-to-issb-and-ifrs-sustainability-disclosure-standards/ — Tier 5.*

**Driver 2 — GHG Protocol Scope 3 standard as the accounting backbone.** CDP's climate questionnaire is built on GHG Protocol methodology. The GHG Protocol Corporate Value Chain (Scope 3) Standard is the accounting framework corporate buyers use to categorise their upstream and downstream emissions. A freight forwarder's emissions appear in the buyer's Category 4 (upstream transportation and distribution) and/or Category 9 (downstream transportation and distribution). This means every large corporate buyer managing Scope 3 under GHG Protocol has a structural reason to request forwarder data.

*Source: GHG Protocol Corporate Value Chain (Scope 3) Standard, GHG Protocol. https://ghgprotocol.org/corporate-value-chain-scope-3-standard — Tier 5, industry body.*

**Driver 3 — ISO 14083 alignment.** ISO 14083 (quantification and reporting of GHG emissions from transport chain operations) is increasingly cited as a contractual requirement in freight tenders. CDP's data collection for transport emissions is directionally consistent with ISO 14083 methodology. Whether CDP questionnaire responses satisfy ISO 14083 contractual requirements is not confirmed in sources reviewed; this requires carrier- and contract-specific legal and technical review.

*Source: ISO 14083, ISO. https://www.iso.org/standard/78864.html — Tier 5; CDP Supply Chain Programme page, CDP Worldwide, accessed April 2026.*

**Conversion trigger:** The programme crosses from voluntary to threshold when a requesting organisation makes CDP disclosure a condition of supplier approval, pre-qualification, or contract renewal. The sources reviewed confirm 270+ buyers are programme members and actively request data from their supplier base. The workspace's specific buyers are not identified in publicly available sources; whether any current or prospective clients are CDP requesting members requires commercial intelligence from the workspace's own account data. **This is an internal research gap, not a regulatory gap.**

---

# Supplier Access and Procurement Reality

**CDP Supply Chain operates two distinct access models that the workspace needs to understand separately.**

**As a disclosing supplier (respondent):** Disclosure is free for suppliers responding to a CDP questionnaire request from a buying member. The workspace, if requested by a corporate client that is a CDP member, responds via the CDP platform at no charge. The questionnaire is available on the CDP platform once a request is issued. There is no licensing fee for the supplier/respondent role.

*Source: CDP Supply Chain Programme page, CDP Worldwide, accessed April 2026. https://www.cdp.net/en/supply-chain — Tier 5, industry body.*

**As a buying member (requesting organisation):** The workspace would pay membership fees at one of three tiers (Standard, Lead, Premium) to access supplier data and send requests. Membership pricing is not publicly listed; CDP directs prospective members to an inquiry form. The workspace would need to contact CDP directly to obtain current pricing.

*Source: CDP Supply Chain Programme page, CDP Worldwide, accessed April 2026. https://www.cdp.net/en/supply-chain — Tier 5, industry body. [Note: pricing not publicly available; confirmed via programme page structure.]*

**No exclusivity, lock-in, or scarcity constraint applies** to the respondent role. Any supplier receiving a request can respond. There is no pilot access model; the platform is commercially available. Lead times for onboarding as a disclosing supplier are driven by questionnaire preparation time, not platform access.

**Data comparability note:** CDP data is standardised across respondents, which is its primary procurement value to buyers. For the workspace, this means that a CDP response is directly comparable to competitors' CDP responses — a double-edged dynamic discussed under Competitive Positioning below.

---

# Operational Fit by Transport Mode and Cargo Vertical

The workspace's primary mode is air, secondary road, tertiary ocean. The CDP climate questionnaire is mode-agnostic in structure but requires mode-specific emissions data within the response. The following applies per mode:

**Air (primary mode):**
Emissions reporting for air freight requires fuel consumption data and emissions factors. CORSIA and EU ETS for aviation are driving emissions measurement infrastructure in this mode. CDP questionnaire responses for air freight emissions are compatible with GHG Protocol Category 4/9 methodology. For workspaces moving live events, film and TV, and high-value automotive cargo by air — all high-urgency, time-critical movements — the emissions intensity per tonne-km is significantly higher than ocean. This means air-primary forwarders will report higher absolute emissions intensities than ocean-primary competitors per unit of cargo, which has implications for how the CDP response is framed. Whether CDP scoring penalises air-mode intensity relative to other modes is not confirmed in sources reviewed; this requires review of the CDP climate questionnaire scoring methodology, which is not reproduced in the source material available.

*Source: CDP Supply Chain Programme page, CDP Worldwide, accessed April 2026. Note: CDP scoring methodology detail not reproduced in programme page; workspace should obtain the current questionnaire and scoring guidance directly from CDP. [Research gap.]*

**Road (secondary mode):**
Road emissions data requirements are consistent with GLEC Framework v3.0 methodology for road transport legs. For workspaces operating road movements in Europe, CSRD-linked data requests may come through CDP alongside or in lieu of direct regulatory reporting.

*Source: GLEC Framework v3.0, Smart Freight Centre. https://www.smartfreightcentre.org/en/about-sfc/news/a-solid-foundation-to-further-accelerate-freight-decarbonization-smart-freight-centre-releases-updated-glec-framework-version-30/ — Tier 4.*

**Ocean (tertiary mode):**
Ocean emissions reporting intersects EU ETS for shipping (in force from 2024) and CII ratings. For the workspace's limited ocean activity, the data collection burden is lower in absolute volume but the regulatory cross-reference complexity is higher.

*Source: CDP Supply Chain Programme page, CDP Worldwide, accessed April 2026 (programme scope); EU ETS for shipping noted for context — specific EU ETS citation not in sources pool reviewed; labeled as cross-reference, not independently sourced in this brief.*

**Cargo vertical notes:**
- **Live events and film and TV:** These verticals involve time-critical, high-frequency air movements. Emissions intensity will be elevated relative to standard freight. If event-production companies or studios become CDP requesting members, the workspace will face questionnaire requests from this vertical. No sourced evidence of event-production companies being current CDP requesting members was found in sources reviewed. [Research gap.]
- **Fine art and luxury goods:** Buyers in these verticals (auction houses, luxury conglomerates) are among the categories where CDP buying membership is plausible, given ESG pressure on luxury sector supply chains. No sourced confirmation of specific buyers in these verticals being CDP requesting members was found. [Research gap.]
- **Humanitarian:** Governmental and intergovernmental purchasing organisations, including the US General Services Administration, are noted by CDP as programme participants. Humanitarian freight — often managed for UN agencies, NGOs, and government bodies — sits in a procurement environment where CDP-style environmental data requests are structurally consistent with existing procurement frameworks. This vertical is the one where CDP questionnaire readiness has the clearest and most directly sourced procurement relevance.

*Source: CDP Supply Chain Programme page, CDP Worldwide, accessed April 2026. Reference to US General Services Administration as a programme participant is CDP's own statement; independent confirmation not available in sources reviewed.*

---

# Competitive Positioning Implications for the Workspace

COMPETITIVE EDGE

**The workspace's competitive position on CDP is determined by data readiness, not programme awareness.** Awareness of CDP among freight forwarders in premium cargo verticals is not tracked in publicly available sources reviewed for this brief. The following competitive analysis is anchored only in sourced structural facts.

**Structural advantage for data-ready forwarders:** CDP questionnaire responses are scored and benchmarked. A forwarder with route-level emissions data (GHG Protocol-compliant, ISO 14083-aligned, GLEC Framework-consistent) will produce a substantively stronger CDP response than a forwarder relying on industry-average emission factors. For the workspace's air-primary operations, investment in accurate flight-level emissions data (rather than default ICAO or IATA average factors) is the single most impactful data readiness move.

*Source: GLEC Framework v3.0, Smart Freight Centre. https://www.smartfreightcentre.org/en/about-sfc/news/a-solid-foundation-to-further-accelerate-freight-decarbonization-smart-freight-centre-releases-updated-glec-framework-version-30/ — Tier 4; GHG Protocol Scope 3 Standard. https://ghgprotocol.org/corporate-value-chain-scope-3-standard — Tier 5.*

**Comparability risk:** Because CDP standardises data across respondents, a premium freight forwarder's CDP response sits in the same dataset as general cargo forwarders. Buyers comparing CDP scores across freight suppliers will see the workspace's score alongside competitors. If the workspace has not invested in granular emissions data, a competitor with better data infrastructure will score more favourably on transparency and data quality, independent of actual emissions performance. This is a structural competitive risk for any forwarder in premium verticals where buyer sophistication is high.

**Named competitor positions:** No sourced data is available in sources reviewed identifying which specific freight forwarding competitors are CDP respondents or their CDP performance scores. CDP public scores are available on the CDP website for organisations that have chosen to make their scores public. The workspace's commercial team should search the CDP public scores database for key competitors as a specific intelligence action. [Research gap — actionable.]

*Source: CDP Supply Chain Programme page, CDP Worldwide, accessed April 2026 (public scores referenced as available). Competitor-specific positions not available in sources reviewed.*

**SBTi alignment as a signal:** Forwarders with validated Science Based Targets will score more strongly on the CDP climate governance and target-setting sections. For the workspace operating across Americas, Europe, and Asia trade lanes with significant air exposure, SBTi target validation provides a defensible and externally verifiable position in client conversations and CDP responses.

*Source: Science Based Targets initiative. https://sciencebasedtargets.org — Tier 5, industry body.*

---

# Conversational and Strategic Talking Points

**What the workspace can credibly say to clients:**

- "We are prepared to respond to CDP Supply Chain questionnaire requests. If your procurement team uses CDP to collect supplier environmental data, we can engage through that process." [Credible only if the workspace has, in fact, prepared its CDP response capability.]
- "Our emissions data is structured around GHG Protocol Scope 3 methodology, which is the accounting basis for CDP's climate questionnaire." [Credible only if the workspace has implemented GHG Protocol-aligned Scope 3 tracking.]
- "For your Scope 3 Category 4 and 9 reporting, we can provide transport emissions data for the movements we handle on your behalf." [Credible only if route-level or shipment-level data is available.]

**Questions to pose to clients:**
- "Is your organisation a CDP Supply Chain member? If so, we can prioritise getting your supplier questionnaire completed accurately."
- "What disclosure frameworks are driving your supply chain data requests — is it CSRD, ISSB/IFRS S2, or internal ESG policy? The answer shapes which data formats we prioritise."
- "Do you require ISO 14083-compliant emissions reporting, or is GHG Protocol methodology sufficient for your current needs?"

**Pitfalls to avoid:**
- Do not claim a CDP score or rating without having one. CDP scores are issued by CDP and cannot be self-attributed.
- Do not claim that responding to the CDP questionnaire constitutes compliance with CSRD or any binding regulation. CDP is a voluntary disclosure platform; its regulatory alignment is indirect, not substitutive. **Legal Confirmation Required** for any claim that CDP disclosure satisfies a specific regulatory obligation.
- Do not characterise CDP savings figures (US$54.4bn) as independently verified — they are CDP's own reported figure.
- Do not claim that ISO 14083 compliance is achieved through CDP disclosure alone; the methodological alignment requires verification against the specific contractual standard cited by the client.

---

# Time-to-Market, Procurement Window, and Action

ACTION REQUIRED

**The procurement window is not time-bounded by a fixed regulatory deadline — it is bounded by the annual CDP disclosure cycle.** CDP runs its questionnaire annually. The 2025 disclosure cycle has concluded; the 2026 cycle is underway per CDP's programme page ("Disclosure Cycle 2026" is referenced). If the workspace receives a CDP questionnaire request from a client in the current cycle and is unprepared to respond substantively, it will miss the cycle and face a one-year gap before the next response opportunity — during which the buyer's supplier record shows either no response or a low-quality response.

*Source: CDP Supply Chain Programme page, CDP Worldwide, accessed April 2026. https://www.cdp.net/en/supply-chain — "Disclosure Cycle 2026" referenced on programme page.*

**Specific actions:**

1. **Determine current CDP exposure.** Query the workspace's key account list against the CDP member list (partially available on the CDP programme page; full membership requires CDP contact). Identify which current and prospective clients are CDP requesting members. This is the single most important intelligence action — without it, preparation effort cannot be targeted. **Owner: commercial/account management team. Timeframe: immediate.**

2. **Audit emissions data infrastructure.** Assess whether current emissions tracking produces GHG Protocol Scope 3-compliant data at shipment or route level for air, road, and ocean movements. If it does not, identify the gap between current state and what a credible CDP climate questionnaire response requires. For air-primary workspaces, flight-level fuel consumption data or carrier-provided emissions intensity data is the critical input. **Owner: operations and sustainability team. Timeframe: before the next client CDP request is received.**

3. **Obtain and review the 2026 CDP climate questionnaire.** The questionnaire is available through the CDP platform to invited respondents. If the workspace has not been invited in 2026 but anticipates requests, it can access CDP's question bank via the CDP disclosure support resources referenced on the programme page. Review the questionnaire structure before receiving a formal request so that data gaps are identified in advance. **Owner: sustainability or compliance function. Timeframe: within 30 days.**

4. **Search CDP public scores for key competitors.** CDP publishes scores for organisations that have chosen public disclosure. Identify whether key freight forwarding competitors have CDP scores, and at what level. This is actionable competitive intelligence available at no cost. **Owner: commercial team. Timeframe: within 30 days.**

5. **Assess SBTi target validation timeline.** If the workspace does not have validated Science Based Targets, the CDP climate questionnaire will reflect a gap on the governance and target-setting sections. SBTi validation is a multi-month process; beginning it now creates lead time advantage if clients begin requiring it in 2026–2027 procurement cycles. **Owner: senior leadership with sustainability and finance. Timeframe: decision within 60 days.**

6. **Do not enrol as a buying member without a clear use case.** Membership as a requesting organisation is appropriate only if the workspace manages a large supplier base from which it needs standardised environmental data at scale. For a freight forwarder primarily acting as supplier rather than buyer, the respondent role (free) is the operative position. Buying membership should be evaluated only if the workspace's own supply chain management strategy requires it. **Owner: commercial and finance. Timeframe: as needed, not urgent.**

---

|---|---|---|---|
| CDP Climate Change Questionnaire and Scoring Methodology | https://www.cdp.net/en/guidance | 5 | Direct source for understanding how CDP scores freight forwarder responses; required to assess competitive positioning on transparency and data quality. |
| CDP Public Scores and A-Lists | https://www.cdp.net/en/scores | 5 | Enables competitive intelligence on whether key freight forwarding peers have CDP scores and at what level; actionable without membership. |

---
prior.intersection_summary: CDP Supply Chain operates as the primary commercial channel through which corporate buying members collect supplier Scope 3 data. Its climate questionnaire is built on [GHG Protocol Corporate Value Chain (Scope 3) Standard], meaning a forwarder's Scope 3 accounting methodology directly determines the quality of any CDP response. [ISO 14083] alignment in emissions data collection improves CDP response quality but does not automatically satisfy ISO 14083 contractual requirements — these are parallel, not identical, obligations. [IFRS/ISSB Sustainability Disclosure Standards] (IFRS S1/S2) are a structural driver of why CDP buying members are expanding supplier data requests: ISSB-aligned reporters need supplier-level emissions data to populate their own disclosures. [Science Based Targets initiative] validation strengthens the CDP climate governance and target-setting sections; a forwarder without SBTi targets will present gaps in those sections that SBTi-validated competitors will not.
